description: Alert and command flow monitoring for slackmgr manager and restapi

panels.description: Number of active per-channel manager goroutines. Red = 0 when there are open issues indicates a manager crash.
panels.title: Active Channel Managers

panels.description: Total open issues across all channels.
panels.title: Total Open Issues

panels.description: Average number of alerts processed per minute across all channels.
panels.title: Alerts / min

panels.description: p95 latency of outbound Slack API calls. Green = fast, yellow = sluggish, red = Slack is slow or unreachable.
panels.title: Slack API Latency (p95)

panels.description: Rate of alerts accepted by the channel manager, broken down by channel.
panels.title: Alert Ingestion Rate by Channel

panels.description: Of alerts matching an existing open issue, how many triggered a Slack update (grouped) vs. were absorbed as duplicates without any update (absorbed). Elevated absorbed rate relative to grouped indicates high alert noise.
panels.title: Alert Grouping vs Absorbed

panels.description: Rate of issue lifecycle events summed across all channels. Created should roughly balance archived over time.
panels.title: Issue Lifecycle Rates

panels.description: Alert queue processing outcomes per second, stacked by result. Non-success results (slack_error, db_error, lock_timeout, gate_timeout, move_race) indicate transient failures requiring investigation.
panels.title: Alert Queue Outcomes

panels.description: Rate of commands executed, broken down by action type. Shows which Slack interactions users are triggering most.
panels.title: Commands Processed by Action

panels.description: Current open issue count per channel, sorted descending. Sized for 20-50 channels.
panels.title: Open Issues per Channel

panels.description: Rate of issue state transitions triggered by user commands or automatic rules.
panels.title: Issue State Events

panels.description: Time spent running the processActiveIssues cycle (archiving, escalation checks) per channel. High values may indicate slow DB queries.
panels.title: Issue Processing Cycle Duration

panels.description: Rate of outbound Slack API calls by method.
panels.title: Slack API Call Rate

panels.description: Latency of individual outbound Slack API calls (p50/p95/p99 across all methods). Elevated p99 indicates Slack-side slowness or network issues.
panels.title: Slack API Call Latency

panels.description: Rate of Slack API errors by action and error type. Any sustained non-zero rate indicates connectivity or permission issues.
panels.title: Slack API Error Rate

panels.description: Rate of Slack API retries after recoverable errors. High retry rate indicates transient Slack failures.
panels.title: Slack API Retry Rate

panels.description: In-process Slack API cache hit ratio by method. Low ratios increase outbound Slack API call volume.
panels.title: Slack Client Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description: Rate of incoming Socket Mode events by type. Useful for understanding event volume and detecting unusual event patterns (e.g. repeated connection errors).
panels.title: Socket Mode Events by Type

panels.description: Times the Slack rate-limit gate was triggered (429 received) in the selected window. Should be 0 during normal operation.
panels.title: Rate Limit Gate Signals (window total)

panels.description: Times the Socket Mode drain wait timed out during rate limit recovery. Should always be 0 — indicates the drain wait is misconfigured or too short.
panels.title: Rate Limit Drain Timeouts (window total)

panels.description: Time that alert processing was blocked waiting for the rate limit gate to open. Elevated values indicate Slack is actively rate limiting the application.
panels.title: Rate Limit Gate Blocked Duration

panels.description: Distribution of Retry-After durations returned by Slack in 429 responses. Shows how long Slack asked us to back off.
panels.title: Slack API Retry-After Duration

panels.description: Time from alert received from queue to ack/nack (p50/p95/p99). High values indicate slow Slack API calls or DB operations under load.
panels.title: Alert Queue Processing Duration

panels.description: Time from command received from queue to ack/nack (p50/p95/p99).
panels.title: Command Queue Processing Duration

panels.description: Rate of non-success alert queue results broken down by failure reason. Any sustained non-zero values warrant investigation.
panels.title: Alert Queue Failures

panels.description: Time to acquire the per-channel distributed lock (p50/p95/p99). High p99 indicates contention between multiple manager instances.
panels.title: Channel Lock Acquire Duration

panels.description: Rate of channel lock acquisition timeouts by channel. Sustained non-zero values cause alerts to be nacked and re-queued.
panels.title: Channel Lock Acquisition Failures

panels.description: Rate of processActiveIssues cycles skipped because another manager instance ran recently. Expected in multi-instance deployments; surprising in single-instance.
panels.title: Issue Processing Cycles Skipped (multi-instance)

panels.description: In-process DB cache hit ratio by operation (issue_hash, move_mapping, processing_state). Low ratios increase DB query load.
panels.title: DB Cache Hit Ratio by Operation

panels.description: Incoming HTTP request rate per second by path and method.
panels.title: HTTP Request Rate

panels.description: HTTP request latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99) per path.
panels.title: HTTP Request Latency

panels.description: Fraction of HTTP requests returning 5xx or 4xx status codes by path. 5xx = server errors, 4xx = client/validation errors (e.g. rate-limited callers get 429).
panels.title: HTTP Error Rate

panels.description: Rate of incoming alert requests rejected with 429 Too Many Requests, broken down by channel. Identifies which channels are triggering rate limiting.
panels.title: Rate-Limited Alert Requests by Channel

title: slackmgr
